channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Iran's Conflict Escalates as Supreme Leader Khamenei Laid to Rest Amid Ceasefire Tensions
item.description: The article details the burial of Iran's slain Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on July 9, 2026, following U.S. airstrikes on February 28. The event coincided with Iranian military strikes on U.S...

item.title: Forty years after homosexual law reform, New Zealand continues to address the legacy of historical convictions
item.description: Forty years after New Zealand passed the Homosexual Law Reform Act 1986, the anniversary is being marked as both a milestone in civil rights and a reminder that unresolved justice issues remain. The l...

item.title: Ekurhuleni officials face corruption charges over fraudulent salary increments and disciplinary obstruction
item.description: Four former Ekurhuleni municipal officials—former manager Imogen Mashazi, head of human resources Linda Gxasheka, and legal advisor Adv Kemi Behari—are charged with corruption and fraud for allegedly ...

item.title: Key Stocks to Monitor: Levi Strauss, Pepsico, and Astrazeneca
item.description: The Wall Street Journal's live coverage highlights three major stocks expected to influence market trends. Levi Strauss & Co. (LEVI) faces challenges from shifting consumer preferences toward athleisu...

item.title: Abdullah Ibrahim's Journey Through Exile, Spirituality, and African Jazz Evolution
item.description: Abdullah Ibrahim, the South African jazz pianist, navigated a complex career marked by exile, spiritual transformation, and musical innovation. His journey began in the 1960s, a period of political tu...

item.title: Jen Psaki Criticizes Graham Platner's Controversial Political Career
item.description: In a recent segment, MS NOW host Jen Psaki addressed the fallout from Graham Platner's controversial Senate campaign in Maine. Platner, a former Democratic candidate, faced escalating scrutiny over al...

item.title: Labour's Andy Burnham Signals Tougher Stance on Israel in Gaza Conflict
item.description: Andy Burnham, the prospective Labour leader, has pledged a more assertive approach towards Israel regarding its actions in Gaza, acknowledging past shortcomings in the party's response. The former Gre...

item.title: OR Tambo Municipality Faces Criticism Over Stalled Toilet Project
item.description: A sanitation project aimed at providing hundreds of toilets to households in Ngquqa and surrounding villages under the OR Tambo District Municipality has been stalled since December 2023. Residents re...

item.title: Nigel Farage's Resignation Sparks Uncontested By-Election in Clacton
item.description: A by-election in the Essex constituency of Clacton has been formally triggered following Nigel Farage's resignation as Reform UK leader and MP. Reform MP Lee Anderson moved the writ in Parliament, set...

item.title: Lagos proposes mandatory waste bins and penalties for illegal dumping
item.description: Lagos State is proposing a new environmental by-law that requires every household to have an approved waste bin. Residents without bins could face fines of ₦50,000 or up to six months in jail. The law...

item.title: Helen Slater reflects on Supergirl's legacy and encourages Milly Alcock to ignore online criticism
item.description: Helen Slater, who starred as Supergirl in the 1984 film, has offered words of encouragement to Australian actor Milly Alcock as she faces online criticism ahead of her portrayal of the iconic superher...

item.title: EDSIEC Prepares for Edo LG Elections with 10,000 Staff and Security Measures
item.description: The Edo State Independent Electoral Commission (EDSIEC) has deployed 10,000 temporary staff to manage the local government elections across the state. The commission chairman, Jonathan Aifuobhokhan, c...

item.title: Texas Bakery Faces Backlash After Criticizing MAGA in Viral Post
item.description: A Texas bakery, Hive Bakery in Flower Mound, sparked national attention after a Facebook post criticizing the MAGA movement went viral. The bakery expressed disappointment in the country's political c...

item.title: US national Matthew Aaron VanDyk seeks court permission to cook his own meals in Tihar Jail, citing health concerns
item.description: US national Matthew Aaron VanDyk, who was arrested by the National Investigation Agency (NIA) in connection with the alleged Myanmar terror training case, has approached a Delhi court seeking permissi...

item.title: Dr. Paul Msoka's Leadership Drives Sustainable Development in Tanzania
item.description: Dr. Paul Msoka has emerged as a key figure in Tanzania's development landscape, showcasing how determination and vision can turn adversity into remarkable achievements. His journey from humble beginni...

item.title: Europe Faces Record Heat and Deadly Heat Dome in June 2026
item.description: The article discusses the unprecedented heatwave that struck Europe in June 2026, marking the warmest June on record for Western Europe according to the Copernicus Climate Change Service. A deadly hea...

item.title: 23-year-old paramedic remembered for kindness and compassion after N.B. collision
item.description: Jenna Croucher, a 23-year-old paramedic from Prince Edward Island, died in a tragic collision between an ambulance and a transport truck in New Brunswick on Tuesday. Her father, Robin Croucher, descri...

item.title: Cronulla Sharks Host Brisbane Broncos in NRLW Round 2 at Ocean Protect Stadium
item.description: The Cronulla Sharks will take on the Brisbane Broncos in Round 2 of the 2026 NRL Women's Premiership at Ocean Protect Stadium in Sydney. This match, scheduled for July 9, 2026, marks a significant eve...

item.title: Trump praises SpaceX's Shotwell for $325M donation to Trump Accounts
item.description: President Donald Trump publicly praised Gwynne Shotwell, CEO of SpaceX, and her husband Robert for their $325 million donation to the Trump Accounts, a tax-advantaged savings account for American chil...

item.title: Former EALA MP Abubakar Zein Dies and Buried in Nairobi After Battle with Illness
item.description: Former East African Legislative Assembly (EALA) member Abubakar Zein passed away at Avenue Hospital in Nairobi on July 7, 2026, after complications from his medical treatment. He was laid to rest at L...
